Looper (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $66,486,205Details
International Box Office $103,980,200
Worldwide Box Office $170,466,405
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $21,092,509
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $12,382,343
Total $33,474,852
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Time travel has been invented—but it is illegal and only available on the black market. When the mob wants to get rid of someone, they send their target 30 years into the past, where a “looper”—a hired gun, like Joe—is waiting to mop up. Joe is getting rich and life is good... until the day the mob decides to “close the loop,” sending back Joe’s future self for assassination.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$20,801,552 (31.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.20 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:39.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,992 opening theaters/2,993 max. theaters, 4.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $99,896,008

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 28th, 2012 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Australia)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Croatia)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Greece)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (New Zealand)
Video Release: December 31st, 2012 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong violence, language, some sexuality/nudity and drug content.
(Rating bulletin 2238, 8/29/2012)
Running Time: 119 minutes

Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction

Production Countries: United States, China
Languages: English

Synopsis

Time travel has been invented—but it is illegal and only available on the black market. When the mob wants to get rid of someone, they send their target 30 years into the past, where a “looper”—a hired gun, like Joe—is waiting to mop up. Joe is getting rich and life is good... until the day the mob decides to “close the loop,” sending back Joe’s future self for assassination.
